title: Experten om techaktier: "Mer uppsida i halvledare än hårdvara"
summary_md:
# Experten om techaktier: "Mer uppsida i halvledare än hårdvara"

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-04-30  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A fund manager from Alfred Berg reviews the latest US tech earnings, noting average revenue up ~20% with even stronger profit growth, but emphasizing that stock reactions hinge on cloud and AI monetization rather than headline beats. The key view is that the real upside lies with AI enablers — semiconductors, memory chips, data storage and datacenter buildout — rather than the hyperscalers themselves, with valuations of the mega-caps now seen as reasonable to attractive.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 GOOGL — Google (Alphabet) | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Cloud surprise drove stock up; market rewarded increased AI investment; fund held overweight all year and views valuation as reasonable.

#### Reasoning

Google was the standout among the mega-caps: its external cloud business, the fastest-growing segment amid surging AI capex, beat expectations and the market positively received plans to invest even more. The manager kept Google as the one mega-cap they were not underweight throughout last year, and now sees valuations across the big names as reasonable to attractive after continued earnings growth.

#### Sell Triggers

- if cloud growth decelerates
- if AI investment fails to monetize
- if valuation re-rates sharply higher

> *"Google överraskade lite grann på sin molntjänst, som är det område som växer egentligen mest just nu"*

---

### 🟡 META — Meta | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Stock fell sharply post-report; internal-only cloud limits external monetization; less clear AI monetization strategy got punished despite raised capex.

#### Reasoning

Meta dropped sharply after earnings because, unlike peers, it has no external cloud business — it uses capacity for its own consumer products — so heavy AI spend (capex guided up to ~135bn USD) does not translate into the same long-term profit leverage. With a less clear monetization strategy, the stock was punished, but the manager notes valuations of mega-caps are generally reasonable and the fund has cut its underweight in the group.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI capex keeps rising without monetization clarity
- if user/ad growth stalls
- if margins compress from spend

> *"Meta tappade kraftigt i efterhanden, medan Googles steg kraftigt"*

---

### 🟡 NVDA — Nvidia | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Neutral | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

The 'spider in the web' of AI; fund traded around it and is now neutral-weighted; mega-cap valuations seen as reasonable to attractive.

#### Reasoning

Nvidia is described as central to the AI buildout ('spindeln i nätet') and now represents ~6% of the world index, up from ~4% a year ago. The fund has moved in and out of the name and currently holds it neutral-weighted. The broader thesis favors AI enablers — semiconductors, memory chips, data storage — over the hyperscalers, suggesting continued semiconductor upside, but the explicit positioning on Nvidia is neutral.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI capex spending slows
- if competition erodes pricing power
- if index weight concentration unwinds

> *"Nvidia får man ju inte glömma såklart, det är ju spindeln i nätet"*

---
